title: Richly Roasted Northern Irish Beet Gratin
description: A romantic and creamy dish featuring roasted beets, this gratin is a perfect representation of Northern Irish cuisine, with a focus on fresh and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients.

ingredients.name: beets
ingredients.notes: any variety, but golden beets add a nice color

instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Wrap the beets in foil and roast for 45 minutes, or until tender.
Let the beets cool, then peel and slice them into 1/4 inch thick rounds.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the garlic until fragrant.
Add the thyme to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
In a separate saucepan, heat the heavy cream over low heat until it starts to simmer.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of beets.
Pour some of the cream mixture over the beets, then sprinkle with cheese.
Repeat the layers until all the ingredients are used, finishing with a layer of cheese on top.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until golden and bubbly.
